page: Amazon Web Services m5n.large is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 1 physical core supporting hyper-threading to deliver 2 vCPUs, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the m5n.large occupies the lower tiers of performance metrics. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance is categorized as weak, while multi-core performance falls into the poor category. Memory bandwidth tests also reflect poor performance, with read-only large block speeds at 13.27 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.41 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET operations at 1.66 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 1.86 GB/sec are situated in the bottom 10% of benchmarked systems.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5n.large offers an entry-level general-purpose resource profile with dedicated CPU allocation, making it suitable for predictable but low-intensity workloads. Given its performance constraints in compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations, this instance is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight background tasks, development environments, or small-scale general-purpose applications that do not require high computational density or rapid memory access.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.large is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz, providing 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, alongside 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics categorized as weak and poor, respectively. Memory bandwidth and database operations also rank in the bottom 10%. Consequently, the m5n.large is qualitatively positioned as a budget-friendly option for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and lightweight general-purpose applications that do not demand high-throughput memory or intensive computational resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for lightweight workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for basic workloads.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual server offering dedicated dual-vCPU processing and balanced memory for entry-level workloads and basic web hosting.

2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR4 system memory
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
